candidates.why: Primary top-line revenue line; matches target exactly and is populated across years.
Revenue-related but clearly only a small component, not total revenue.
Also a valid top-line total revenue label; useful as fallback if label changes across years.
Non-core other income component, not total revenue.

candidates.row_label: Interest income from financial assets at amortized cost
candidates.why: Financial income, not operating revenue.
Financial income, not operating revenue.

candidates.row_label: Income Available to Com Excl ExtraOrd
candidates.why: Net income measure, not revenue.
OCI reclassification item, not revenue.

candidates.row_label: Total comprehensive income for the period
candidates.why: Comprehensive income, not revenue.
Operating profit metric, not revenue.
Balance sheet asset, not revenue.
Balance sheet liability, not revenue.

variables.notes: Used the reported top-line labels as a priority list; later code can take the first non-missing value.

candidates.why: Best match for direct production/service costs; broad cost-of-sales style line above operating profit.
Direct materials component, but narrower than the broader cost line.
Operating overhead component, not COGS.

candidates.row_label: Expense relating to short-term leases (included in materials and services)
candidates.why: Only a subcomponent of materials and services, not full COGS.
Inventory asset on the balance sheet, not expense.
Debt-related metric, not COGS.

variables.notes: Selected the broad direct cost line rather than narrower components.

candidates.why: Could be overhead in some cases, but here it sits within the materials/services area and looks more like direct operating/service cost support than SG&A.
Typical overhead/personnel cost component and a strong SG&A candidate.

candidates.row_label: Stock-Based Compensation in Selling, General & Administrative Expenses
candidates.why: Explicit SG&A-related overhead component.
Dedicated R&D line; usually included in XSGA only if not already embedded in broader selected overhead bucket.
Semantically SG&A-related, but the row is a supplemental disclosure and appears unpopulated in preview.
Explicit SG&A label exists and should be preferred when present, even if preview is empty.
R&D-related label but appears unpopulated and less specific than the expense line.

final_choice.row_label: Stock-Based Compensation in Selling, General & Administrative Expenses

variables.notes: Explicit SG&A row exists ('Selling, General & Admin') but appears empty in the preview, so per rules it is included along with broader overhead coverage. The candidate list clearly misses the key populated overhead bucket 'Other expenses', so I selected it from the full preview. 'Employee benefit costs' and explicit SG&A stock-based compensation were also included as overhead components. I did not add 'Research & Development Expense' here because it appears likely embedded within the selected 'Other expenses' subtotal, and adding it would risk double counting. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Income Statement] Other expenses

candidates.why: Dedicated R&D expense line above pretax income and within operating expenses; strongest match.
R&D-related label but appears as an unpopulated supplemental/disclosure row rather than the main expense line.

variables.notes: Used the dedicated R&D expense line as primary choice, with the alternate label as fallback.

candidates.why: Broad total financial expense line, but includes non-interest items and write-downs, so less preferred than granular recurring components.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
candidates.why: Recurring interest component and should be included in financing costs.
Core recurring interest expense line; strongest component for XINT.
Valid conceptually but unpopulated in preview; useful only as fallback.
Derivative valuation/hedging item, not recurring interest expense.
Likely recurring financing-related cost and can be included when granular interest components are used instead of total finance cost.

candidates.row_label: Non-controlling interests Earnings per share from profit attributable to the owners of the parent (in euro per share)
candidates.why: EPS-related line, not interest expense.
Attribution of profit, not interest expense.
Attribution/equity item, not interest expense.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental duplicate of lease interest; avoid double counting versus main row.
Balance sheet liability, not expense.
Equity item, not interest expense.
Equity item, not interest expense.

final_choice.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities

variables.notes: Preferred granular recurring finance cost components and excluded broad totals and non-recurring/valuation items.

candidates.row_label: Non-controlling interests Earnings per share from profit attributable to the owners of the parent (in euro per share)
candidates.why: EPS attribution line, not equity.
Income statement profit attribution, not balance sheet equity.

candidates.row_label: Cash flow hedges recorded in equity
candidates.why: OCI reserve movement, not total book equity.

candidates.row_label: Share of other comprehensive income of investments accounted for using the equity method
candidates.why: OCI component, not total equity.
Large equity component but excludes share capital, so not total owner equity.
Only one reserve component, not total equity.
Total equity includes non-controlling interests here, so using it would conflict with separate MIB mapping.

variables.notes: The candidate list misses the best row for owner equity excluding NCI. In the equity section, 'Total' equals parent/shareholders' equity, while 'Total equity' includes 'Non-controlling interests' in years where NCI is non-zero. Therefore I selected 'Total' from the full preview to avoid double counting with MIB. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Balance Sheet] Total

candidates.row_label: Non-controlling interests Earnings per share from profit attributable to the owners of the parent (in euro per share)
candidates.why: EPS-related line, not balance sheet minority equity.
Income statement profit attribution, not book minority equity.
Income statement OCI attribution, not preferred for balance sheet MIB.
Direct balance sheet NCI line; best match for MIB.
Equivalent balance sheet label and useful as fallback if terminology changes.

variables.notes: Used the balance sheet NCI labels as a priority list.
